items.docs: Lists the Apigee organizations that are available to the caller. Returns a list of organization names and metadata.

params.name: parent
params.description: Required. Name of the Google Cloud project in which to associate the Apigee organization. Pass the information as a query parameter using the format: projects/{project}.

items.docs: Creates an Apigee organization. The organization is the top-level container for all Apigee resources including API proxies, products, developers, and environments.
Retrieves the properties for an Apigee organization, including configuration settings, subscription type, and metadata.
Updates the properties for an Apigee organization. No other fields in the organization profile will be updated.
Deletes an Apigee organization. This is a long-running operation that deletes all resources associated with the organization.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: Maximum number of items to return per page.

params.name: pageToken
params.description: Page token returned from a previous list request.

items.docs: Lists the names of all API proxies in an organization. The list includes information about the API proxy revisions available.

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the API proxy. Must be unique within the organization.

params.name: action
params.description: Action to perform when importing an API proxy configuration bundle. Set to "import" when uploading a ZIP bundle.

items.docs: Creates an API proxy. The API proxy created will not be accessible at runtime until it is deployed to an environment. Provide the proxy configuration as an API proxy bundle or as an inline configuration.
Gets an API proxy including a list of existing revisions, the proxy metadata, and the last modified date.

params.name: updateMask
params.description: Required. List of fields to update. Use comma-separated field names from the resource. Example: "labels".

items.docs: Updates an existing API proxy metadata such as labels. Does not update the proxy bundle or revision content.
Deletes an API proxy and all associated endpoints, policies, resources, and revisions. The proxy must be undeployed from all environments before it can be deleted.
Lists all revisions for an API proxy. Returns an array of revision numbers in string format.

info.name: Apigee Get an API Proxy Revision

params.name: revisionId
params.description: Revision number of the API proxy.

items.docs: Gets an API proxy revision. Returns the configuration and metadata for the specified revision of an API proxy.

info.name: Apigee Delete an API Proxy Revision

params.name: revisionId
params.description: Revision number of the API proxy.

items.docs: Deletes an API proxy revision. The revision must be undeployed from all environments before it can be deleted.

params.name: attributename
params.description: Name of the attribute used to filter the search.

params.name: attributevalue
params.description: Value of the attribute used to filter the search.

params.name: expand
params.description: Set to true to get expanded details about each API product.

params.name: count
params.description: Number of API products to return in the API call.

params.name: startKey
params.description: Name of the API product from which to start displaying the list of API products.

items.docs: Lists all API product names for an organization. Filter the list using an attribute name and value query parameter.
Creates an API product in an organization. API products bundle API resources for consumption by developers. An API product can contain API proxies and quota settings.
Gets the configuration for an API product. The API product name is required in the request URL.
Updates an existing API product. The full API product object must be included in the request body, even if only a subset of properties are being updated.
Deletes an API product from an organization. Deleting an API product causes apps that are associated with the product to be unable to access the API resources defined in that product.

params.name: expand
params.description: Set to true to return expanded developer details.

params.name: startKey
params.description: Email of the developer from which to start the list.

params.name: app
params.description: Filter by the name of the app owned by the developer.

items.docs: Lists all developers in an organization by email address. Returns a list of developer email addresses or expanded developer objects.
Creates a developer in an organization. A developer is a consumer of APIs, typically represented by a person or company who registers apps and obtains API keys.

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

items.docs: Returns the developer profile by email address or developer ID. Includes information about the developer apps and custom attributes.

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

items.docs: Updates a developer with the properties specified in the request body. The full developer object must be included in the request body.

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

items.docs: Deletes a developer from the organization. All apps and API keys associated with the developer are also removed. All pending push notifications are also deleted.

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

params.name: expand
params.description: Set to true to return full app details.

params.name: count
params.description: Number of developer apps to return.

params.name: startKey
params.description: Name of the developer app from which to start the list.

items.docs: Lists all apps created by a developer in an organization. Returns an array of app IDs or expanded app objects.

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

items.docs: Creates an app associated with a developer. This API associates the developer app with the specified API product and auto-generates an API key for the app to use in calls to API proxies inside that product.

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

items.docs: Returns the details for a developer app, including the API keys and API products associated with the app.

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

items.docs: Updates the attributes of the developer app. This API replaces the current attributes with those specified in the request body.

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

items.docs: Deletes a developer app. This operation also removes all API keys and associations to API products for the app.

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

items.docs: Returns a list of all API keys for a developer app, including consumer keys and secrets.

info.name: Apigee Create a Developer App Key

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

items.docs: Creates a custom consumer key and secret for a developer app. Useful when migrating existing consumer keys and secrets to Apigee.

info.name: Apigee Get a Developer App Key

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

params.name: keyId
params.description: Consumer key of the developer app.

items.docs: Returns details for a consumer key for a developer app, including the key, its status, and the associated API products.

info.name: Apigee Update a Developer App Key

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

params.name: keyId
params.description: Consumer key of the developer app.

items.docs: Adds an API product to a developer app key, enabling the app to access the API resources bundled in the API product.

info.name: Apigee Delete a Developer App Key

params.name: developerId
params.description: Email address or ID of the developer.

params.name: keyId
params.description: Consumer key of the developer app.

items.docs: Deletes an app's consumer key. After the consumer key is deleted, it cannot be used to access any APIs.
Lists all environments in an organization. Returns a list of environment names or expanded environment objects.
Creates an environment in an organization. Environments provide a runtime execution context for API proxies, with separate settings for caches, target servers, keystores, and more.
Gets the profile for an environment in an organization, including properties, state, and deployment type.
Updates an existing environment. The full environment object must be included in the request body.
Deletes an environment from an organization. Before deleting, all API proxies must be undeployed from the environment.

info.name: Apigee Deploy an API Proxy Revision

params.name: revisionId
params.description: Revision number of the API proxy.

params.name: override
params.description: Set to true to force deployment of the new revision over the currently deployed revision by overriding conflict checks.

params.name: sequencedRollout
params.description: Set to true to enable sequenced rollout, which deploys to pods in a gradual manner.

items.docs: Deploys a revision of an API proxy to an environment. After deployment, the API proxy revision can accept requests from clients.

info.name: Apigee Undeploy an API Proxy Revision

params.name: revisionId
params.description: Revision number of the API proxy.

params.name: sequencedRollout
params.description: Set to true to enable sequenced rollout for undeployment.

items.docs: Undeploys an API proxy revision from an environment. After undeployment, the API proxy revision will no longer accept requests from clients.

params.name: sharedFlows
params.description: Set to true to return shared flow deployments instead of API proxy deployments.

items.docs: Lists all deployments across all environments in an organization, including the API proxy name, revision, and environment.
Lists all target servers in the specified environment of an organization. Target servers define backend service endpoints.
Creates a target server in the specified environment. Target servers decouple TargetEndpoint configurations from concrete backend URLs.
Gets the configuration of a target server in the specified environment.
Updates an existing target server in the specified environment. The full target server object must be included in the request body.
Deletes a target server from an environment.
Lists the key value maps in the specified environment.

info.name: Apigee Create a Key Value Map

items.docs: Creates a key value map in the specified environment. Key value maps store data for retrieval at runtime by policies.

info.name: Apigee Delete a Key Value Map

params.name: keyValueMapId
params.description: Name of the key value map.

items.docs: Deletes a key value map from the specified environment.
Lists all shared flows in an organization. Shared flows contain reusable logic that can be invoked from API proxy flows.

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the shared flow. Must be unique within the organization.

params.name: action
params.description: Set to "import" when uploading a ZIP bundle.

items.docs: Uploads a ZIP-formatted shared flow configuration bundle. The bundle is imported into the organization as a new shared flow.
Gets a shared flow including a list of existing revisions.
Deletes a shared flow and all associated policies, resources, and revisions. The shared flow must be undeployed from all environments.

params.name: dimensionName
params.description: Name of the dimension to query. Common values include apiproxy, developer_email, developer_app, access_token, and others.

params.name: select
params.description: Comma-separated list of metrics. Example: sum(message_count), avg(total_response_time).

params.name: timeRange
params.description: Time range for the query in the format MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM~MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM.

params.name: timeUnit
params.description: Granularity of the time series (second, minute, hour, day, week, month).

params.name: filter
params.description: Filter expression to narrow the results.

params.name: sortby
params.description: Comma-separated list of columns to sort the results.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of records to return.

items.docs: Retrieves metrics for API proxy performance in an environment. Provides traffic, latency, error counts, and other analytics data across specified time ranges.
Lists the data stores available for exporting analytics data.

info.name: Apigee Create an Analytics Data Store

items.docs: Creates a data store for exporting analytics data from an organization.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: Maximum number of items to return per page.

params.name: pageToken
params.description: Page token returned from a previous list request.

items.docs: Lists all app groups in an organization. App groups organize developers and their applications.
Creates an app group in an organization. App groups provide a way to organize developers and their applications.
Returns the app group details for a specified app group.
Updates an app group. The full app group object must be included in the request body.
Deletes an app group from an organization.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: Maximum number of items to return per page.

params.name: pageToken
params.description: Page token returned from a previous list request.

items.docs: Lists all Apigee runtime instances for the organization.
Creates an Apigee runtime instance. This is a long-running operation that provisions the infrastructure for API runtime.
Gets the details for an Apigee runtime instance, including state, host, port, and location.
Deletes an Apigee runtime instance. This is a long-running operation that deprovisions the runtime infrastructure.
